rulings.Case Title: DR. DOE VS. KAISER PERMANENTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel John E. Kincaid Produce Records In Response To Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 2, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANTS KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, and THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Compel John E. Kincaid Produce Records In Response To Subpoena. KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS and THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Compel John E. Kincaid To Produce Records In Response To Subpoena to which no opposition has been filed is GRANTED. The subpoena is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and, specifically, the records are relevant to subjects put at issue by Plaintiff. Defendants notified Plaintiff of the subpoena and request for records, and Plaintiff has not objected. Kincaid's blanket objection based on the psychotherapist-patient privilege is overruled. (See CCP, Sec. 1985.3.) The records are excepted from the privilege (see Evid. Code, Sec. 1016) and/or Plaintiff waived the privilege when he put at issue his emotional condition and treatment by Kincaid and failed to object to the subpoena (see In re Lifschutz (1970) 2 Cal.3d 415, 433-434). Kincaid shall produce the records within 10 days of notice of this order. Defendants must maintain the records consistent with the parties' protective order. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Amended Notice Of Motion & Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 2, 2025, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment. Plaintiff Jay Epps's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment, filed December 9, 2024, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted and judgment shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the [Proposed] Consent Judgment. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
